content.problemStatement:
Chef has a special string called the parenthesisStringparenthesisStringparenthesisString that contains only three types of characters: `'('`, `')'`, and `'*'`. Chef wants to determine if this parenthesisStringparenthesisStringparenthesisString is valid according to some strict rules. The rules are:


Every `'('` must have a matching `')'` that comes after it.
Every `')'` must have a matching `'('` that comes before it.
The character `'*'` can represent either `'('`, `')'`, or an empty string.
Help Chef by writing a program that checks whether the given parenthesisStringparenthesisStringparenthesisString can be considered valid under these conditions.

Function DeclarationFunction NameisValidParenthesisStringisValidParenthesisStringisValidParenthesisString – This function checks whether a given parenthesis string containing '(', ')', and '' can be considered valid by interpreting '' as '(', ')', or an empty string.

Parameters
parenthesisStringparenthesisStringparenthesisString : A string consisting only of characters '(', ')', and '*', representing Chef’s parenthesis string.
Return Value
Returns truetruetrue if the parenthesis string can be valid.
Returns falsefalsefalse otherwise.
Constraints
1≤T≤1051 \leq T \leq 10^51≤T≤105
1≤∣parenthesisString∣≤1001 \leq |parenthesisString| \leq 1001≤∣parenthesisString∣≤100
Each character of parenthesisStringparenthesisStringparenthesisString is either `'('`, `')'`, or `'*'`.

Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases.
For each test case:

The next line contains a string parenthesisStringparenthesisStringparenthesisString.




Output Format

For each test case, print truetruetrue if the parenthesis string is valid, or falsefalsefalse otherwise.
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S consisting only of characters '(', ')', and '*'.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "true" if the string can be interpreted as a valid parenthesis sequence, otherwise output "false".

sampleTestCases.explanation: All strings can be turned into a balanced parenthesis sequence by choosing appropriate replacements for '*'.
1) Starts with ')', impossible to balance. 2) Single '*', can be empty, yielding a valid empty string. 3) No assignment of '*' makes the number of '(' match the number of ')'.

solution.approach: Traverse the string while maintaining two counters: low = minimal possible number of unmatched '(' and high = maximal possible number of unmatched '(' at the current position. For '(' increase both counters. For ')' decrease both (but low cannot go below 0). For '*' treat it as either '(' or ')' or empty: increase high (as '(') and decrease low (as ')') if low>0. If at any point high becomes negative, there are too many ')' and the string is invalid. After processing all characters the string is valid iff low == 0 (we can close all '(') and high >= 0. This greedy scan works in O(|S|) time and O(1) extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(|S|) per test case, total O( Σ|S| ) ≤ O(T·100).
